method: Strict semantic clustering: same central claim, causal mechanism, and practical implication. Shared theme alone is not redundant.
selection_rule: Within a confirmed cluster, keep the newest post and propose removal of older posts.
removal_scope: Local website notes only. Preserve LinkedIn and X publications and preserve full anti-duplication memory.
confirmation_method: Independent record-by-record full-text comparison after semantic clustering.

remove.topic_thesis: Collaboration, mission or quality, and play are distinct channels for generating Ren.
remove.hook: When one person can do what ten used to do, trust stops being a byproduct of work.

keep.topic_thesis: The Impatience of Jobs is the problem of compressing relational time without building a cult.
keep.hook: You can compress work with AI. You cannot compress the bushel of salt it takes for people to trust each other.

decision.justification: Both notes argue that AI and distributed work reduce the trust once generated through shared work and that ritual cannot manufacture Ren. However, the remove post adds a materially distinct operating framework: Ren must be cultivated through three separate channels—real collaboration, commitment to mission or quality, and unscored play—and warns that none can substitute for the others. The keep post instead emphasizes that relational time cannot be compressed. Those lead to different operator decisions, so removal is not safe.
